name: Creation Of Pod In System Namespace
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Leo Tsaousis (@laripping)
SIGMA Title: Creation Of Pod In System Namespace
SIGMA Description: Detects deployments of pods within the kube-system namespace, which could be intended to imitate system pods.
System pods, created by controllers such as Deployments or DaemonSets have random suffixes in their names.
Attackers can use this fact and name their backdoor pods as if they were created by these controllers to avoid detection.
Deployment of such a backdoor container e.g. named kube-proxy-bv61v, could be attempted in the kube-system namespace alongside the other administrative containers.


False positives:
- System components such as daemon-set-controller and kube-scheduler also create pods in the kube-system namespace

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%create%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%pods%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%kube-system%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Creation Of Pod In System Namespace on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event matches LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%create%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%pods%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%kube-system%' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Creation Of Pod In System Namespace
